# 360-Degree Signal Taxonomy

Use this taxonomy to avoid solving from a narrow slice of evidence.

## 1. Direct Pain Signals

- complaints
- support tickets
- incident reports
- bug reports
- crisis logs
- user-submitted failures

These show what is hurting now.

## 2. Frontline Observation

- field observations
- operator notes
- moderator notes
- staff escalations
- case worker patterns
- maintainer retrospectives

These reveal workarounds, bottlenecks, and hidden friction.

## 3. Outcome Signals

- drop-off
- relapse
- rework
- repeat failure
- churn
- service degradation
- safety events
- missed targets

These connect symptoms to real consequences.

## 4. Structural Signals

- ownership fragmentation
- policy friction
- funding constraints
- governance gaps
- conflicting incentives
- dependency chokepoints

These explain why a problem reproduces.

## 5. Research and Expert Signals

- academic reports
- benchmark studies
- peer-reviewed findings
- standards
- sector frameworks
- expert testimony

These help distinguish local noise from durable patterns.

## 6. Direction-of-Travel Signals

- industry shifts
- demographic changes
- technology adoption
- regulatory trajectory
- community expectations
- emerging norms

These prevent recommendations that are already being made obsolete.

## 7. Counter-Signals

- contrary field evidence
- outlier data
- disagreement from credible actors
- strategic noise
- brigading
- duplicated anecdotes

These are necessary to test whether a loud narrative is actually true.

## 8. Intervention Evidence

- prior pilots
- policy trials
- comparable case studies
- program evaluations
- postmortems

These help estimate whether an intervention is realistic, not just attractive.
